summary: The reproduction bundle generates a trusted root and a serverAuth-only leaf certificate, re-signs an existing catalog, and demonstrates that Signify accepts the catalog and flat file without enforcing the code-signing EKU.
expected_security_impact: The run should show that a catalog signed by a certificate with only the serverAuth EKU is accepted by Signify's catalog verification path and can successfully validate a flat file, while the same signer fails once code_signing is explicitly required.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned signify at 25e7046ba148c468a96acbc5a18ea5aec52044eb, built the Docker image, generated a trusted root plus a serverAuth-only leaf certificate, re-signed the existing oem89.cat catalog, and successfully verified amdacpbtscoext.inf with that catalog. The run printed leaf_ekus=['server_auth'], default_ctl_verify=success, explicit_code_signing_check=failure, flat_file_verify=success, and the final proof string EVOHUNT_EKU_BYPASS_CONFIRMED.
verification_method: The helper script verifies four conditions: the generated leaf certificate does not contain the code_signing EKU, CertificateTrustList.verify succeeds by default, the same catalog fails when extended_key_usages=["code_signing"] is enforced, and flat-file verification succeeds with the re-signed catalog. Success is signaled by the exact proof string EVOHUNT_EKU_BYPASS_CONFIRMED.
evidence_produced_by_run: The container printed leaf_ekus=['server_auth'], proving the catalog signer lacked the code_signing EKU.
The container printed default_ctl_verify=success, proving Signify accepted the catalog signer anyway.
The container printed explicit_code_signing_check=failure, proving that enforcing code_signing rejects the same signer and isolating the missing EKU requirement as the root cause.
The container printed flat_file_verify=success and EVOHUNT_EKU_BYPASS_CONFIRMED, proving end-to-end flat-file verification succeeded with the attacker-signed catalog.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
